context: This chapter details the specific gifts and donations to be made at the time of death and immediately after, including the Vaitarani cow donation for crossing the dreadful river, golden image donation, bed donation (shayyaa-dana), and other charitable acts that aid the departed soul on its journey through the afterlife.

verses.hindi:
गरुड उवाच —
मृत्युकाले तु यद् दानं कर्तव्यं तद् वदस्व मे ।
केन दानेन मुक्तिः स्यात् प्रेतत्वं केन नश्यति ॥
verses.transliteration:
Garuḍa uvāca —
mṛtyu-kāle tu yad dānaṃ kartavyaṃ tad vadasva me |
kena dānena muktiḥ syāt pretatvaṃ kena naśyati ||
verses.translation: Garuda said: Tell me what gifts should be made at the time of death. By which gift is liberation obtained? By which is the state of being a ghost destroyed?

verses.hindi:
श्रीभगवानुवाच —
श्रृणु तार्क्ष्य प्रवक्ष्यामि दानं मृत्युसमीपगम् ।
यद्दत्त्वा मानवो मृत्युं तीर्त्वा स्वर्गतिमाप्नुयात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
Śrī Bhagavān uvāca —
śṛṇu Tārkṣya pravakṣyāmi dānaṃ mṛtyu-samīpagam |
yad dattvā mānavo mṛtyuṃ tīrtvā svarga-gatim āpnuyāt ||
verses.translation: The Blessed Lord said: Listen, O Tarksya, I shall describe the gifts to be given near death. Having given which, a man, crossing over death, attains the heavenly path.

verses.hindi:
वैतरणी महाघोरा यमस्य नगरे स्थिता ।
तां तर्तुं दुर्लभं मर्त्यैः पापकर्मरतैर्नरैः ॥
verses.transliteration:
Vaitaraṇī mahā-ghorā Yamasya nagare sthitā |
tāṃ tartuṃ durlabhaṃ martyaiḥ pāpa-karma-ratair naraiḥ ||
verses.translation: The Vaitarani, most terrible, situated in the city of Yama — it is difficult for mortals engaged in sinful deeds to cross it.

verses.hindi:
विण्मूत्ररुधिरैः पूर्णा मांसशोणितकर्दमा ।
केशकीटसमाकीर्णा भयङ्करतरा नदी ॥
verses.transliteration:
viṇ-mūtra-rudhiraiḥ pūrṇā māṃsa-śoṇita-kardamā |
keśa-kīṭa-samākīrṇā bhayaṅkara-tarā nadī ||
verses.translation: Filled with excrement, urine, and blood, its mud made of flesh and gore, swarming with hair and worms — that river is most terrifying.

verses.hindi:
तस्या उत्तरणार्थं तु वैतरणीं महापुमान् ।
गां ददाति ततः प्राज्ञो गवा तीर्णा च वैतरणी ॥
verses.transliteration:
tasyā uttaraṇārthaṃ tu Vaitaraṇīṃ mahā-pumān |
gāṃ dadāti tataḥ prājño gavā tīrṇā ca Vaitaraṇī ||
verses.translation: For the crossing of that river, a wise and great man gives a cow — the Vaitarani cow. By means of that cow the Vaitarani is crossed.

verses.hindi:
कृष्णवर्णां पयस्विनीं सवत्सां सर्वकामदाम् ।
ताम्रपात्रोपरिस्थां तु सोपस्करसमन्विताम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
kṛṣṇa-varṇāṃ payasvinīṃ sa-vatsāṃ sarva-kāma-dām |
tāmra-pātropari-sthāṃ tu sopaskara-samanvitām ||
verses.translation: A black cow, milch, with her calf, granting all desires, standing upon a copper vessel, furnished with all accessories.

verses.hindi:
वस्त्रयुग्मेन संवेष्ट्य सुवर्णेन विभूषिताम् ।
सकाञ्चनखुरां गां तां विप्राय प्रतिपादयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
vastra-yugmena saṃveṣṭya suvarṇena vibhūṣitām |
sa-kāñcana-khurāṃ gāṃ tāṃ viprāya pratipādayet ||
verses.translation: Wrapped with a pair of garments, adorned with gold, with golden hooves — that cow should be presented to a Brahmana.

verses.hindi:
इमां गां प्रतिगृह्णीष्व वैतरणीसमुत्तरे ।
वैतरणीमहानद्यां प्लवो भव ममानघ ॥
verses.transliteration:
imāṃ gāṃ pratigṛhṇīṣva Vaitaraṇī-samuttare |
Vaitaraṇī-mahā-nadyāṃ plavo bhava mamānagha ||
verses.translation: The donor should say: 'Accept this cow for the crossing of the Vaitarani. Be a raft for me in the great river Vaitarani, O sinless one.'

verses.hindi:
गवा पुच्छं समालभ्य तर्यते वैतरणी नदी ।
तस्मात् वैतरणीदानं सर्वदानोत्तमोत्तमम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
gavā pucchaṃ samālabhya taryate Vaitaraṇī nadī |
tasmāt Vaitaraṇī-dānaṃ sarva-dānottamottamam ||
verses.translation: By holding the tail of the cow, the river Vaitarani is crossed. Therefore the Vaitarani gift is the best of the best of all gifts.

verses.hindi:
शय्यादानं प्रवक्ष्यामि श्रृणु तार्क्ष्य समाहितः ।
शय्या प्रदीयते यस्मात् प्रेतस्य सुखकारिणी ॥
verses.transliteration:
śayyā-dānaṃ pravakṣyāmi śṛṇu Tārkṣya samāhitaḥ |
śayyā pradīyate yasmāt pretasya sukha-kāriṇī ||
verses.translation: I shall describe the gift of a bed — listen attentively, O Tarksya. The bed is given because it brings comfort to the departed soul.

verses.hindi:
पलङ्कं रचयेत् सम्यग् वस्त्रोपधानभूषितम् ।
सोपस्करं सकम्बलं तूलिकासहितं शुभम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
palaṅkaṃ racayet samyag vastropādhāna-bhūṣitam |
sopaskaraṃ sa-kambalaṃ tūlikā-sahitaṃ śubham ||
verses.translation: A bed should be properly prepared, adorned with cloth and pillows, furnished with accessories, blankets, and auspicious mattresses.

verses.hindi:
ताम्बूलपूगमादाय कर्पूरागरुचन्दनैः ।
धूपदीपैश्च संयुक्तां शय्यां विप्राय दापयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
tāmbūla-pūgam ādāya karpūrāgaru-candanaiḥ |
dhūpa-dīpaiś ca saṃyuktāṃ śayyāṃ viprāya dāpayet ||
verses.translation: With betel, areca nut, camphor, aloe, and sandalwood, together with incense and lamps, the bed should be given to a Brahmana.

verses.hindi:
शय्यां प्रदाय विप्राय यो वदेन्मन्त्रमुत्तमम् ।
इयं शय्या मया दत्ता मम प्रेतसुखावहा ॥
verses.transliteration:
śayyāṃ pradāya viprāya yo vaden mantram uttamam |
iyaṃ śayyā mayā dattā mama preta-sukhāvahā ||
verses.translation: Having given the bed to a Brahmana, one should recite this excellent mantra: 'This bed is given by me for the comfort of the departed soul.'

verses.hindi:
सौवर्णं पुरुषं कृत्वा दशाङ्गुलसमन्वितम् ।
वस्त्रसंवेष्टितं सम्यक् ब्राह्मणाय निवेदयेत् ॥
verses.transliteration:
sauvarṇaṃ puruṣaṃ kṛtvā daśāṅgula-samanvitam |
vastra-saṃveṣṭitaṃ samyak brāhmaṇāya nivedayet ||
verses.translation: A golden image of a person, ten fingers in measure, properly wrapped in cloth, should be presented to a Brahmana.

verses.hindi:
हिरण्यगर्भो भगवान् एष विष्णुरिति स्मृतः ।
हिरण्यदानेन नरो विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति ॥
verses.transliteration:
hiraṇya-garbho bhagavān eṣa Viṣṇur iti smṛtaḥ |
hiraṇya-dānena naro Viṣṇu-lokaṃ sa gacchati ||
verses.translation: The Lord Hiranyagarbha is known as Vishnu. By the gift of gold a man goes to the world of Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
घृतधेनुं प्रदातव्यां घृतपूर्णां सवत्सकाम् ।
घृतं वै पावनं लोके घृतदानं सुखप्रदम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
ghṛta-dhenuṃ pradātavyāṃ ghṛta-pūrṇāṃ sa-vatsakām |
ghṛtaṃ vai pāvanaṃ loke ghṛta-dānaṃ sukha-pradam ||
verses.translation: A ghee-cow (a cow-shaped vessel filled with ghee) should be given, along with its calf-vessel. Ghee is purifying in this world; the gift of ghee brings happiness.

verses.hindi:
गुडधेनुं तिलधेनुं जलधेनुं च दापयेत् ।
रसधेनुं मधुधेनुं शर्करा धेनुमेव च ॥
verses.transliteration:
guḍa-dhenuṃ tila-dhenuṃ jala-dhenuṃ ca dāpayet |
rasa-dhenuṃ madhu-dhenuṃ śarkarā-dhenum eva ca ||
verses.translation: One should also give the jaggery-cow, sesame-cow, water-cow, juice-cow, honey-cow, and sugar-cow (vessels shaped like cows filled with these substances).

verses.hindi:
सप्तधान्यानि देयानि ब्राह्मणेभ्यो विशेषतः ।
व्रीहियवतिलाः मुद्गा माषकङ्गुप्रियङ्गवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
sapta-dhānyāni deyāni brāhmaṇebhyo viśeṣataḥ |
vrīhi-yava-tilāḥ mudgā māṣa-kaṅgu-priyaṅgavaḥ ||
verses.translation: The seven grains should be given especially to Brahmanas: rice, barley, sesame, mung beans, black lentils, millet, and priyangu.

verses.hindi:
पादुकां छत्रदण्डं च कमण्डलुमथापि वा ।
आसनं भाजनं वस्त्रं प्रदद्याद् ब्राह्मणे शुभे ॥
verses.transliteration:
pādukāṃ chatra-daṇḍaṃ ca kamaṇḍalum athāpi vā |
āsanaṃ bhājanaṃ vastraṃ pradadyād brāhmaṇe śubhe ||
verses.translation: Sandals, an umbrella and staff, a water-pot, a seat, a vessel, and clothing should be given to an auspicious Brahmana.

verses.hindi:
यमस्य लोकं गच्छन्ति ये मृता दानवर्जिताः ।
तेषां न सुखमस्तीह न परत्र कदाचन ॥
verses.transliteration:
Yamasya lokaṃ gacchanti ye mṛtā dāna-varjitāḥ |
teṣāṃ na sukham astīha na paratra kadācana ||
verses.translation: Those who die without gifts go to the world of Yama. For them there is no happiness here or hereafter, ever.

verses.hindi:
दानहीनस्य मर्त्यस्य प्रेतत्वं जायते ध्रुवम् ।
तस्मात् सर्वप्रयत्नेन दानं कुर्वीत मानवः ॥
verses.transliteration:
dāna-hīnasya martyasya pretatvaṃ jāyate dhruvam |
tasmāt sarva-prayatnena dānaṃ kurvīta mānavaḥ ||
verses.translation: For a mortal devoid of gifts, the state of being a ghost surely arises. Therefore, with every effort, a man should make charitable gifts.

verses.hindi:
अमावास्यापितृतिथौ दानमन्नं विशेषतः ।
पितृभ्यो दीयते यत्तु तच्छान्तिकरमुत्तमम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
amāvāsyā-pitṛ-tithau dānam annaṃ viśeṣataḥ |
pitṛbhyo dīyate yat tu tac chānti-karam uttamam ||
verses.translation: On the new moon day, the day sacred to the ancestors, gifts of food especially should be given. That which is given to the ancestors brings supreme peace.

verses.hindi:
एवं दत्त्वा विधानेन सर्वदानानि भक्तितः ।
मुक्तिं प्राप्नोति पुरुषो विष्णुसायुज्यमाप्नुयात् ॥
verses.transliteration:
evaṃ dattvā vidhānena sarva-dānāni bhaktitaḥ |
muktiṃ prāpnoti puruṣo Viṣṇu-sāyujyam āpnuyāt ||
verses.translation: Having thus given all gifts according to prescribed rites with devotion, a person obtains liberation and may attain unity with Vishnu.

verses.hindi:
इदं रहस्यं परमं मया तार्क्ष्य प्रकीर्तितम् ।
मरणे यः पठेन्नित्यं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
verses.transliteration:
idaṃ rahasyaṃ paramaṃ mayā Tārkṣya prakīrtitam |
maraṇe yaḥ paṭhen nityaṃ sa yāti paramāṃ gatim ||
verses.translation: This supreme secret has been proclaimed by Me, O Tarksya. He who reads this constantly at the time of death attains the highest destination.
